codeReview.summary: Reviewed commit afd23d2 and found the implementation satisfies VAL-VOLUME-001/002/003/004/005/008: build_canonical_lead_dataset now calls owner enrichment with max_lookups=2000, Sunbiz with top_n=100, voter enrichment with top_n=1000; build_pre_permit_leads now queries all MIAMI_DADE_ZIPS with limit_per_zip=100; and fetch_parcels_by_zip now defaults to limit_per_zip=100, iterates all passed ZIPs, and forwards resultRecordCount=limit_per_zip to the API request. Added tests/test_enrichment_caps.py exercises these cap values and configurability checks for the relevant function parameters.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-backend skill does not explicitly call out how to patch lazy imports (`from module import symbol` inside function bodies), which caused avoidable patch-target debugging during this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-30T20-34-56-516Z__raise-enrichment-caps__63ba9651-2159-4cd0-a1b5-1fa5f44049fc.json includes skillFeedback.suggestedChanges to document this mock.patch gotcha; transcript skeleton for worker session 63ba9651-2159-4cd0-a1b5-1fa5f44049fc shows repeated investigation around patching lazy imports before proceeding.

summary: I reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit diff for raise-enrichment-caps and found the feature behavior and tests aligned with the required VAL-VOLUME assertions, with no blocking or non-blocking code defects identified.
